steps.title: Set up the right triangle and identify the legs
steps.content: In triangle $XYZ$, the right angle is at $Y$, which means the two legs are $XY$ and $YZ$. We're given that $YZ = 22$ units and angle $Z = 39°$. To find the area, we need both legs, so we must find the length of $XY$.

steps.title: Apply the tangent ratio from angle Z
steps.content: From angle $Z$ (39°), the opposite side is $XY$ and the adjacent side is $YZ = 22$. Using the tangent ratio: $$\tan 39° = \frac{XY}{YZ} = \frac{XY}{22}$$ Solving for $XY$: $$XY = 22 \tan 39°$$

steps.title: Calculate the area and extract k
steps.content: The area of a right triangle is: $$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times XY \times YZ = \frac{1}{2} \times 22\tan 39° \times 22 = \frac{1}{2} \times 484 \times \tan 39° = 242\tan 39°$$ Comparing this with the given form $k \tan 39°$, we have $k = 242$.
